metric_value: Traditional storage architectures will soon become inadequate for Global 2000 enterprises
notes: As high-data-volume applications come online and service levels rise, server-attached storage will fail to scale.

metric_value: Traditional DAS architectures were largely displaced by NAS/SAN in Global 2000 enterprises by 2005
notes: NAS and SAN became standard enterprise storage by mid-2000s. DAS was relegated to specialized use cases.

metric_value: Enterprises that fail to adopt network storage will overpay for storage
notes: Hill argues non-adoption of network storage leads to paying too much for storage.

metric_value: Server-dependent storage leads to extreme labor-intensive management difficulties
notes: Backup, data movement, and storage management become unmanageable without server-independent network storage.

metric_value: NAS and SAN became dominant enterprise storage; cloud storage extended the model to internet scale
notes: NetApp, EMC Celerra (NAS); IBM, HP (SAN) dominated enterprise storage. AWS S3 (2006) scaled network storage to cloud.

metric_value: New network storage facilities should have greater server independence than previous generations
notes: Core architectural principle: storage independent from servers enables better throughput, flexibility, manageability.

metric_value: Throughput as key requirement for network storage architecture
notes: One of three requirements Hill identifies for network storage architecture.

metric_value: Flexibility as key requirement for network storage architecture
notes: One of three requirements Hill identifies for network storage architecture.

metric_value: Manageability as key requirement for network storage architecture
notes: One of three requirements Hill identifies for network storage architecture.

metric_value: Most Global 2000 enterprises using decades-old storage architectures as of 1997
notes: Aberdeen characterizes the problem as widespread among largest enterprises.

metric_value: New high-data-volume applications will trigger storage architecture failures
notes: Hill cites new applications raising the service level bar as the trigger event for architecture obsolescence.

metric_value: AWS S3 launched 2006 extending network storage to cloud scale; validates server-independent storage model
notes: Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service) launched March 2006, proving network/server-independent storage at internet scale.
